# Contributing to Atryum

### How to contribute

All contributions are welcome and appreciated! Thank you!

You can email the devs directly at atryum-devs@validmind.ai with any questions about the project or the contribution process.

## Contributing Bug Reports

Add bug reports as [github issues](https://github.com/validmind/atryum/issues).

## Contributing Code

1. Follow the development process outlined in README.md
2. Prepare fork of this repository and branch with your fix in it.
3. Open a Pull Request against this repository.
4. Sign the CLA.
5. Be courteous when discussing the pull request with maintainers and other community members.

## On AI Code

The Atryum project encourages developers to use ai. However, quality is required when submitting pull requests. As with any open source project, the best way to get a large feature in is by first fixing a small issue to get comfortable with the project, and to get maintainers comfortable with you.

To aid this, we reccomend you provide the maximum context possible in the pull request body. This includes human-written description of what you are trying to do, and what problems it fixes, and also includes the prompts you gave your agent when developing the patch, as well as a full development agent log (if available, shoutout amp for making this easy).

## Security

For serious security issues, do not report on github issues - email atryum-devs@validmind.ai directly.

Thank you for contributing to Atryum!
